hazard.summary: OpenAI Codex retry path ignores Retry-After while same-repo Gemini path handles it, causing retries inside the provider cooldown window
signals: openai-codex-responses.ts uses pure exponential backoff for 429 responses — 1s, 2s, 4s — with no Retry-After header consulted.
The Gemini provider in the same repo handles this correctly: reads Retry-After, HTTP-date format, body patterns, and retryDelay JSON field.
When OpenAI returns Retry-After: 60, all three retries fire inside the cooldown window and fail. Retry budget exhausted. Agent run stalls.
PR #5859 fixed Codex 429 retry delays to honor server retry hints and added focused regression test coverage.

constraints: 429 retry logic must consult Retry-After before computing local exponential backoff.
Retry-After must be treated as the minimum retry delay for the affected provider path.
Provider-specific retry implementations must not diverge when a correct retry primitive already exists in the same codebase.
Fallback exponential backoff may run only when server retry hints are absent or unparsable.
verification: Simulate an OpenAI Codex 429 response with Retry-After: 60. Assert the next retry delay is at least 60 seconds, not 1, 2, or 4 seconds.
Simulate a 429 response with HTTP-date Retry-After. Assert the retry delay is parsed and honored as a floor.
Simulate a 429 response without Retry-After. Assert the existing exponential backoff fallback still applies.
Run the focused regression test added in PR #5859 and the relevant provider build/test suite.

impact.operator_impact: Agent runs stall because all retry attempts are consumed inside the provider cooldown window.
impact.business_consequence: Concurrent sessions amplify failed retries under sustained load, increasing user-visible failures and wasting provider quota without improving recovery.

notes: This is a clean same-repo divergence receipt: the Gemini provider already had the correct Retry-After handling, but the OpenAI Codex path used local exponential backoff. The reusable failure is not absence of a primitive; it is a correct primitive unwired at a sibling provider path. Issue #5677 was approved for agent work and PR #5859 opened with a fix and regression test. RESOLUTION: First corpus finding implemented by autonomous agent. Human triage approval by jeremymcs (Flux Labs / PatchDeck). PR #5859 merged with 154 lines and regression tests within minutes of approval.

constraints: Retry-After must be consulted before local exponential backoff.
Correct provider retry primitives must be reused across sibling provider paths.
Server-directed cooldown windows must not be violated by local retry schedules.
anti_patterns: retry attempts fired inside provider cooldown window
